name: United States
summary: Day 34. TRUMP PRIMETIME ADDRESS — FIRST PUBLIC EXIT TIMELINE: '2-3 weeks' to finish war with 'extremely hard' hits. Claimed Iran's president asked for ceasefire — Iran immediately denied. 13 US KIA, 348 WIA acknowledged. Threatened destruction of ALL Iran electric plants and oil sites. Attacked NATO — threatened withdrawal. European NATO allies actively blocking: Spain closed airspace, Italy denied Sicily base, France blocked overflights, Germany 'not our war.' 28% Republican disapproval driving political calculus — MAGA media fractured (Carlson/Bannon anti-war vs Hannity/Levin pro-war). Fox Ingraham cracked then fell back in line within 24 hours. April 6 energy pause 4 days away with NO extension signaled — if expired, US resumes strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure. Gas $4.126/gallon. SPR 243M barrels (1984 low). DOE issued 10M barrel RFP.

signals.content: Trump gave order for Operation Epic Fury. CENTCOM began airstrikes using B-2, B-1, B-52 bombers, Tomahawks, HIMARS. Targeted Iran's military, nuclear, and leadership infrastructure.
War cost: $11.3B+ in first 6 days (~$2B/day). 13 US soldiers killed. Pentagon began drafting $200B supplemental budget request.
Pentagon requested $200B from Congress. Hegseth: 'Takes money to kill bad guys.' GOP resistance emerging.

Trump said considering 'winding down' military efforts. Simultaneously deployed 2,500 additional Marines. Axios reported considering Kharg Island occupation. Lifted some Iran oil sanctions (140M barrels to market).
Trump issued 48-hour ultimatum to Iran: reopen Hormuz or US strikes power plants.

Trump posted on Truth Social claiming 'productive conversations' and announced 5-day postponement of strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure. Pentagon ordered pause on power plant strikes. This directly contradicts his 48-hour ultimatum.

Trump claimed 15 points of agreement with Iran including that Tehran will 'never have a nuclear weapon,' with April 9 as target date for ending conflict. Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff leading negotiations, with intermediaries Turkey, Egypt, and Pakistan facilitating. Islamabad proposed as meeting venue. CRITICAL: CENTCOM confirmed US forces continue aggressive strikes on non-energy Iranian targets — the pause applies ONLY to power plants/energy infrastructure. Axios reported Kharg Island ground invasion under 'serious consideration' simultaneously with peace planning — both escalation and diplomatic tracks active. CIA and DIA testified in classified hearing that Iran is in a 'deep command and control crisis' — Mojtaba Khamenei's whereabouts remain a mystery (no Nowruz address, only written statement). National Security Advisor Waltz says US is 'using Iran's own oil strategy against them.' US struck Iran-backed armed group HQ in Iraq's Anbar province.

Trump extended energy strike pause to April 6, claiming 'as per Iranian government request' and 'talks going very well.' Iran denied making any request. Pakistan FM confirmed publicly that messages being relayed through Pakistan. US envoy Witkoff said 'strong signs' of deal. Gap between US 15-point plan (nuclear rollback, Hormuz reopening) and Iran's 5-point counterproposal (sovereignty, reparations) is enormous. (Sources: CNBC, Axios, NPR, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Pakistan FM)

2,500 Marines arrived on USS Tripoli (31st MEU from Japan) — speculation centers on Kharg Island seizure operation. 15+ US troops wounded at Prince Sultan Air Base, total war casualties past 300. VP Vance on podcast: war will continue 'a little while longer' to ensure Iran 'neutered for a very long time'; called gas prices 'temporary.' US acknowledged war may extend past initial 4-6 week timeline. Trump escalated NATO criticism at Saudi FII conference: 'US no longer needs NATO,' called alliance 'cowards' for refusing to escort ships through Hormuz. 8 million Americans in 'No Kings' anti-war protests across all 50 states. (Sources: CENTCOM, Time, Military.com, Times of Israel, Fortune, WaPo, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera)

Trump claimed Iran agreed to 'most of' 15-point demands (Air Force One Sunday). Pentagon preparing 'weeks of limited ground operations' in Iran — 82nd Airborne + 2 MEUs deploying alongside USS Tripoli Marines. Kharg Island seizure still under consideration. Trump to FT: 'Maybe we take Kharg Island, maybe we don't.' Contradiction: diplomacy claims + ground invasion prep. (Sources: CNN, Al Jazeera, Axios, CNBC, FT)

EVENING UPDATE: Trump FT interview published Monday — 'my favourite thing is to take the oil in Iran but some stupid people back in the U.S. say why are you doing that.' Called objectors 'stupid people.' Repeated Kharg Island seizure consideration. Also told Times of Israel that killing Iran's leaders 'truly is regime change' and a deal 'could be soon.' Claimed Iran agreed to let '20 boats' through Hormuz as 'sign of respect' — unverified by Iran. The 18-hour arc from 'agreed to most' (Sunday) to 'take the oil' (Monday) caused a $12/bbl oil swing. Trump is now the single largest driver of oil volatility — his own contradictory statements moved Brent more than any military action this week. US futures were up 0.6% pre-market on diplomacy hopes before the FT interview. (Sources: FT, Bloomberg, CNN, NBC News, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, CNBC)

MORNING UPDATE: Trump posted on Truth Social threatening to 'completely obliterate' all of Iran's electric generating plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and 'possibly all desalinization plants' if Hormuz not 'immediately' reopened. Called the regime 'A NEW, AND MORE REASONABLE, REGIME.' International law experts flagged this as describing collective punishment prohibited under IHL. 82nd Airborne troops have ARRIVED in Middle East (confirmed by Military Times) — division-level HQ deployed, implying planning for operations larger than brigade-level. 31st MEU (2,200 Marines) from Japan on USS Tripoli arrived in region. 11th MEU from California 3-4 weeks out. Total force building to ~55,000+. IDF struck 170 targets in 24 hours 'throughout Tehran' — unnamed Israeli official: 'no intention to scale back' before talks. April 6 energy pause deadline is 6 days away with no visible progress on conditions.

82nd Airborne confirmed in theater with division-level HQ (Military Times, Stars and Stripes). 31st MEU (2,200 Marines, USS Tripoli) from Japan arrived. Total force ~55,000+. Pentagon preparing 'weeks of limited ground operations' including Kharg Island raids (WaPo, citing officials) — but White House says no authorization given. WSJ BOMBSHELL: Trump told aides willing to end war even if Hormuz remains shut. Leavitt subsequently called Hormuz reopening 'not a core objective.' This complicates ground invasion rationale. 8 war powers votes failed in Congress. House Democrats delayed next vote until mid-April (after recess). Iran VP Aref: 'no one returns from hell.' IDF struck 170 targets throughout Tehran in 24 hours including Isfahan nuclear site (~540 kg 60%-enriched uranium). US struck Tehran power infrastructure causing blackout. CONFIRMED (multiple independent sources).

Trump stated US could end Iran war in 'two to three weeks' — new public off-ramp providing timeline the market can price. However, Pentagon simultaneously preparing 'weeks of limited ground operations' including Kharg Island raids (WaPo). 82nd Airborne remains in theater with division-level HQ. The rhetoric-action gap: public de-escalation signals alongside military escalation preparation. Congressional war powers challenge dead in both chambers (House 212-219, Senate 53-47). Pakistan confirmed on record 'US-Iran indirect talks are taking place' — Rubio-Araghchi meeting in Pakistan could happen 'within days.' (Sources: US News, WaPo, Pakistan FM)

Fox News' Laura Ingraham questioned whether Trump 'fully grasped the complexity' of attacking Iran. Media Matters reported Fox's 'united front in support of Trump's Iran war may be breaking down.' When Fox's prime-time hosts crack, the political base is wobbling. Combined with 8M Americans in 'No Kings' anti-war protests. Political sustainability of the campaign is eroding even as military capability expands. (Sources: Media Matters, The Hill)

IRGC threatened 18 named US companies including Apple, Microsoft, Google, Meta, Nvidia, JPMorgan, Boeing, Tesla with deadline tonight 20:00 Tehran time. Represents new escalation category — targeting private-sector corporate infrastructure. S&P 500 rallied +2.91% despite threat, driven by Pezeshkian peace signal. (Sources: Fars News, Tasnim, The Hill)

WaPo national security desk reported Gulf allies privately urging Trump to continue fighting 'until Iran is decisively defeated.' UAE described as 'perhaps the most hawkish,' pushing for ground invasion. Kuwait and Bahrain also favor continued military action. Oman and Qatar favor diplomatic resolution. Gulf states simultaneously suffering from war AND lobbying for more war — see 'historic opportunity to cripple Tehran.' REPORTED (anonymous sourcing, single outlet but high-credibility WaPo national security desk). (Sources: WaPo, Bloomberg)

Trump delivered first primetime address to the nation on the war (April 1 evening). Key claims: 'core strategic objectives nearing completion,' vowed to finish war in '2-3 weeks' with 'extremely hard' hits. Claimed Iran's president asked for ceasefire — Iran's FM Baghaei immediately called this 'false and baseless.' Acknowledged 13 US KIA and 348 WIA. Threatened to destroy ALL Iran's electric plants and oil sites. Attacked NATO allies — threatened withdrawal, told allies to 'get your own oil.' (Sources: CBS, NPR, CNBC, NBC, White House)

April 6 energy strike pause deadline is 4 days away with NO extension signaled. Trump's rhetoric inconsistent with extending pause ('extremely hard' hits, '2-3 weeks'). If deadline expires without extension, US resumes strikes on Iranian power plants, refineries, energy infrastructure — likely spiking Brent above $110-120. (Sources: Al Jazeera, CNBC, Bloomberg)

European NATO allies actively blocking US military operations: Spain closed airspace entirely, Italy denied Sicily airbase access, France blocked overflight for Israel-bound supply flights, Germany stated 'not our war.' Trump threatened NATO withdrawal. Unprecedented — member states obstructing another member's military operations. (Sources: US News, Bloomberg, Washington Post)
US gas prices $4.126/gallon national average. SPR at 243M barrels — lowest since 1984. DOE issued new 10M barrel RFP April 1. (Sources: EIA, DOE)

28% of Republicans now disapprove of the war per CNN poll — driving Trump's political calculus and likely explains the '2-3 weeks' off-ramp language. MAGA media fractured: Carlson/Bannon (anti-war populist) vs Hannity/Levin/Fox establishment (pro-war). Fox's Ingraham questioned Trump's war understanding, pivoted back under pressure within 24 hours. (Sources: CNN, The Hill)

edges.context: Leading military campaign. 82nd Airborne in theater. Trump primetime address: '2-3 weeks' exit, 'extremely hard' hits. Claimed ceasefire request — Iran denied. Acknowledged 13 KIA, 348 WIA. NATO allies blocking operations (Spain, Italy, France, Germany). April 6 energy pause 4 days away, no extension signaled. Both diplomatic exit and military escalation tracks active.

Joint operations since Feb 28. IDF struck 170 targets in Tehran April 1-2 including steel plants and pharmaceutical facilities. US-Israeli targeting expanding to industrial and civilian infrastructure. Trump's '2-3 weeks' exit timeline may accelerate Israeli strikes as Netanyahu ordered escalation 'in case war ends soon.'
$200B war cost + oil inflation = Fed trapped. War spending compounds existing fiscal concerns. Powell called oil impact 'temporary' — market skeptical.

Trump primetime: threatened destruction of ALL Iran electric plants and oil sites. April 6 pause expiry without extension = US resumes energy strikes, Brent likely spikes $110-120. Gas $4.126/gallon. SPR at 243M barrels (1984 low). DOE issued 10M barrel RFP. '2-3 weeks' off-ramp = bearish; 'extremely hard hits' + April 6 expiry = bullish.

War costs, fiscal deficit, IRGC corporate threats to 18 US companies, NATO fracture all weighing on confidence. S&P rallied +2.91% on peace signal but IRGC corporate deadline produced single AWS strike — market discounting threats. 28% Republican disapproval + Fox crack = political sustainability eroding.

trigger_points.condition: US attempts ground invasion of Kharg Island or Iranian territory
trigger_points.mechanism: Ground troops in Iran = massive escalation. Costs explode, casualties rise. Axios confirmed this is under 'serious consideration.'
De-escalation signal. If credible, could lead to ceasefire talks, Hormuz reopening, oil crash back toward $80s.
RESOLVED: Trump extended pause to April 6 on March 26. New deadline replaces this trigger.

trigger_points.condition: April 6 pause deadline expires without deal
trigger_points.mechanism: If no progress by April 6, US resumes strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure (power plants, refineries). Oil likely spikes above $120. Iran may escalate Hormuz enforcement.
